# Intent Discovery — exploratory intent formation through action

> Users often approach tasks without fully-formed goals. This strategy helps users discover their intent by presenting structured options, observing preferences, and progressively concretizing a hierarchy of intents through iterative exploration.

## Contents

- [Parameters](#parameters)

## Parameters {#parameters}

| key | type | required | description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ctx.concretize_tokens` | number | optional | Max tokens for concretization (default 500) |
| `ctx.max_rounds` | number | optional | Maximum exploration rounds (default 3) |
| `ctx.n_options` | number | optional | Number of options to present per round (default 3) |
| `ctx.surface_tokens` | number | optional | Max tokens for option generation (default 600) |
| `ctx.task` | string | **required** | Initial (possibly vague) user request (required) |

fields.doc: Whether exploration ended early via CONVERGENCE:YES or empty remaining
fields.name: converged

fields.doc: Per-round record of options, key_dimension, and user preference
fields.name: exploration_log

fields.doc: The fundamental choice these options represent (empty if not emitted)
fields.name: key_dimension

fields.doc: Option description (empty string when the LLM emitted title without a dash-separated description)
fields.name: description

fields.doc: Option label parsed from the LLM output (e.g., 'A', 'B', or numeric)
fields.name: label

fields.doc: REMAINING block from the concretize LLM output
fields.name: remaining

fields.doc: RESOLVED block from the concretize LLM output
fields.name: resolved

fields.doc: Updated understanding of intent after this round
fields.name: understanding

fields.doc: Number of exploration rounds actually executed
fields.name: rounds
